title: ABC谋杀案
summary:
Members of a special task force meet at Poirot's house to discuss the investigation into the murders of an old woman, a young girl, and an elderly man. The group consists of Tora Grey, Megan Barnard, Mary DeLour, Franklin Clarke, and Donald Fraser. Poirot explains his theory that the killer is a premeditated actor who scouted locations such as Andover and Bexhill, and that the perpetrator could be either a man or a woman.

Poirot begins questioning the members regarding their recent observations. Franklin Clarke recounts his day, noting that he went fishing, ate Irish stew, and drove to Peardon to mail letters; during his travels, he observed a woman in a striped silk skirt, two children with a fox terrier, and a blonde girl swimming. Tora Grey states that she spent her time performing needlework and handling Sir Clement's letters before retiring early.

Megan Barnard recalls seeing her sister, Betty, two weeks prior. Megan notes that Betty had mentioned being short of money after purchasing a hat and dresses, and that Betty expressed a dislike for a café worker named Milly Higley. When Poirot asks if Betty mentioned meeting a man, Megan responds coldly. Donald Fraser, who had been waiting for Betty at a café on the night of her death, insists he does not remember seeing anyone specific on the beach.

Mary DeLour shares that her aunt had sent her a letter two days before the crime, mentioning she was being bothered by an "old devil." Mary expresses grief over the lost opportunity to celebrate her birthday with her aunt. Franklin Clarke responds by reflecting on the sadness of small, lost things, citing a memory of a woman killed by a car immediately after buying new shoes.
